title: Antony Munoz Email Sequence
text_content:
Email #1
Subject: 
Retire By Treating Life Like A Video Game, (First Name)... 
Preview: 
The same programming that got you here is now your prison...
Body
Have you ever played a video game and noticed the background characters?
The NPCs (Non-Player Characters).
They walk the same path every day. 
Say the same lines. 
Follow their code perfectly.
-Wake up
-Coffee 
-Emails 
-Meetings 
-Gym
-Sleep 
-Repeat
They're rich NPCs, yes…
But still NPCs.
Here's what hit me at 22 after cashing in on crypto… 
I'd won the money game... 
But I was still following someone else's script.
The morning routines... 
The goal-setting… 
The life hacks...
All of it was just fancy NPC code for high achievers.
If you want to see the system I built to escape, click here. 
(First name), there are only two types of people:
NPCs - Following society's code (even the rich ones) 
Players - Writing their own game code
And most millionaires are just NPCs with a bit more money than the average person. 
They chase money.
They chase small goals. 
They follow the same path as most people. 
But they never realize they're playing someone else's game.
The shift from NPC to Player isn't about working harder or making more money.
It's about installing a completely different system.
One that turns your life into a game YOU'RE actually designing.
Not as a metaphor. 
Where you earn XP for real-world tasks. 
Where your calendar becomes a mission list. 
Where every choice either levels you up or keeps you stuck.
I spent 2 years building this system after burning out at 22.
Now I'm semi-retired at 24. Not because I "made it…" 
But because I changed what "it" means.
If you're ready to turn your life into a game YOU'RE actually designing...
Book a call to see if you qualify for The Game of Lyfe
(We only take 20 players per month).
-Antony
P.S. You get one life. One game. And right now, you're playing with someone else's controller. Use the system I used to escape and click here.
